{"credits": [{"type": "remediation_developer", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/49032?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/jelmer/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/jelmer/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/jelmer/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/jelmer/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/jelmer", "id": 49032, "login": "jelmer", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjQ5MDMy", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/jelmer/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/jelmer/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/jelmer/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/jelmer/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/jelmer/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/jelmer", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-47734", "cvss": {"score": 5.7, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:R/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 5.7, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:R/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-400", "name": "Uncontrolled Resource Consumption"}, {"cwe_id": "CWE-789", "name": "Memory Allocation with Excessive Size Value"}], "description": "## Impact

An uncontrolled-resource-consumption (memory exhaustion) denial-of-service vulnerability (CWE-400 / CWE-789).

A client with push access could push a tiny crafted thin pack (~174 bytes)  whose delta header declares a huge   dest_size. When dulwich ingested it via  add_thin_pack / apply_delta, it would  allocate hundreds of MB of memory based on that attacker-controlled size, with no relationship to the actual bytes received.

Who is impacted: Operators running a Dulwich-based Git server that exposes git-receive-pack (i.e. accepts pushes) -
for example via dulwich.server functionality, the HTTP  smart server, or anything built on ReceivePackHandler. 

## Patches

Patched in 1.2.5.

add_thin_pack now accepts a max_input_size keyword (bytes; 0/None = unlimited, matching git's semantics), and ReceivePackHandler reads receive.maxInputSize from the repository config and passes it through. Wire reads are counted and a PackInputTooLarge exception is raised once the cap is exceeded - equivalent to git index-pack --max-input-size.

Users should upgrade to Dulwich 1.2.5 or later and set receive.maxInputSize in their server's repository config to a sane bound for their environment.

## Workarounds

On unpatched versions, receive.maxInputSize has no effect, so it cannot be used as a workaround. Until upgrading, operators should:

- Restrict dulwich-receive-pack (push) access to trusted, authenticated clients only, or disable it entirely on servers that only need to serve fetches.
- Run the server under an OS-level memory limit (e.g. ulimit, cgroups/MemoryMax, or a container memory limit) so a malicious push is killed rather than taking down the host.

##  Resources

- git's receive.maxInputSize / git index-pack --max-input-size documentation 
- Reported by Liyi, Ziyue, Strick, Maurice and Chenchen @ University of Sydney", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00188, "percentile": 0.08506}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-xrvj-v92f-53gj", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-06-08T23:43:42Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-xrvj-v92f-53gj", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-xrvj-v92f-53gj"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-47734"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-06-10T23:16:48Z", "published_at": "2026-06-08T23:43:42Z", "references": ["https://github.com/jelmer/dulwich/security/advisories/GHSA-xrvj-v92f-53gj", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-47734", "https://github.com/jelmer/dulwich/releases/tag/dulwich-1.2.5", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-xrvj-v92f-53gj"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/jelmer/dulwich/security-advisories/GHSA-xrvj-v92f-53gj", "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/jelmer/dulwich", "summary": "Dulwich has unbounded memory allocation in receive-pack from crafted thin packs", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-11T14:07:19Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-xrvj-v92f-53gj", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "1.2.5", "package": {"ecosystem": "pip", "name": "dulwich"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 0.1.0, < 1.2.5"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

reasons: 資産不一致または判定情報不足

title: Dulwich has unbounded memory allocation in receive-pack from crafted thin packs
